name: Meta Ad Library: Search & Enumerate Ads
summary: Search the public Meta Ad Library by keyword or by advertiser and enumerate matching ads (library id, started date, media type, ad copy) into a source-discriminated manifest.
description: Reads the public Meta Ad Library (facebook.com/ads/library) using Meta's documented URL parameters. mode=keyword does a keyword+country search; mode=advertiser resolves the advertiser's Facebook Page id (from the keyword result, or an explicit page_id) and scopes to view_all_page_id so all ads belong to that advertiser. Scrapes ad cards from the rendered DOM (parsed by the stable 'Library ID' / 'Started running on' / 'Sponsored' text anchors), scroll-accumulating up to a configurable cap. Pure read. Emits a manifest validating against schema/ads-manifest-v1.json with source=meta_ad_library.

query.description: Search term: a keyword (mode=keyword) or an advertiser name (mode=advertiser). Recorded in the manifest query.

mode.description: 'keyword' = keyword+country search. 'advertiser' = scope to one advertiser's ads (resolves the Page id from the query, or uses page_id).

country.description: ISO country code the Ad Library requires before searching (e.g. US, GB, IN). Recorded in the manifest query as region.

page_id.description: Advertiser Facebook Page id for precise advertiser scoping (mode=advertiser). When set, skips name resolution.

cap.description: Maximum ads to collect. Scroll-accumulates and stops cleanly at the cap; truncated=true when more were available.

help.summary: Search the Meta Ad Library by keyword or advertiser and list matching ads.

parameters.page_id: Advertiser Facebook Page id for precise advertiser scoping.
parameters.cap: Max ads to collect (default 30, hard cap 300).

examples.command: rzn-browser run meta_ad_library search --param query="running shoes" --param country=US
rzn-browser run meta_ad_library search --param query="Nike" --param mode=advertiser

examples.description: Precise advertiser scope by Page id
examples.command: rzn-browser run meta_ad_library search --param query="Nike" --param mode=advertiser --param page_id=15087023444 --param cap=50

returns: Object: { source, query, mode, cap, count, truncated, advertiser_name, advertiser_id, ads:[{ id, advertiser_id, advertiser_name, media_type, first_shown, started_at, last_shown, copy, url, preview_url }] }.
notes: Uses Meta's documented Ad Library URL parameters (the search box is a non-fillable React component); scrapes ad cards from the rendered DOM.
started_at/first_shown are ISO dates parsed from the card's 'Started running on' text; last_shown is null (Meta cards show only the start date).
media_type is image/video/text from the card content; copy is the ad's primary text.
advertiser mode resolves the Page id from the keyword result (most frequent page_id) unless page_id is given; all returned ads then belong to that advertiser.
No CDP; all JS runs in the page main world.
